transcript.clean_text: a hot day destination bilbao stage five of la welter well on stage four into laguardia pretty much roglic had gone into the red jersey something that had been shared amongst his team since the race began modest advantage of 13 seconds as we started to ponder what lay ahead on a much bigger distance of 187 kilometers and some of the big names were wondering whether they might indeed dare to dream of being part of a breakaway which was bound to form there was a tailwind time to get the picnic ready because this was going to be a battle it was high paced start 50 kilometers per hour for the first 45 minutes that tailwind added to the impetus and at the end of the day a loop circuit including the viveiro climb to be taken twice the intermediate sprint coming late it was the finish line bravest of all mark solaire he bridged over to an already well established breakaway 18 riders pushed on teammates rudy muller and jake stewart for group armor francis easier first task well done but they had to see off the challenge from fred wright if they were going to steal the red jersey from jumbo visma meanwhile another jersey the polka dots was in the mind of l'angelotti from burgos bh he julie took as many points as he could during the day and it was enough to lead that standings by the line lawson credit the american time trialist was also in determined mood constantly ebbing and flowing he was to make the steal the first time over the viveiro angelotti close at hand while they came into town the intermediate sprint was of no consequence the fans though were screaming as they usually do so passionate in this part of the world passion is one thing but determination and efficacy is entirely another jake stewart pushed on leaving rudy mollards to just trace fred wright in that battle for the red jersey in the end jake was to suffer cramp late on and it wasn't to be leaving rudy mollard wondering whether he could snatch the red jersey solaire had other ideas he was struggling at first on the first passage over the viveira but then re-found himself bridging over to stuart and pushing on setting a gap that we wanted would it be bridged it seemed so tenuous it seemed inevitable the chase on the final descent into town was spirited a gap of over four minutes back to the peloton jake stewart out of contention molar tracing fred wright and solaire up the road the gap trimmed at its shortest down to six seconds but solaire believed he attacked he continued to pace home meanwhile those behind him were wondering about the duty of chasing him fred wright didn't want to be anyone's fool but in the end they stared each other into the abyss and solaire was to make it home the sprint ensued and behind solaire as he had time to celebrate it was to be daryl impe that crossed the line behind him third place for fred wright rudy millar in fourth place ahead of lawson credit aunt langelotti the new king of the mountains pronsky mulberger adria and then we waited for the peloton to join the party the atmosphere exceptional here in bilbao an exceptional winner as well solaire usually on duty of course for others today helped himself before second margin was nothing to him it was all about the stage win and the jersey lent of course but for how long to rudy milan peloton coming in five minutes down mullah into red and tomorrow everybody metaphorically will be in the red as the serious climbing begins for now rudy mallard leads fred wright by two seconds fred wright himself going into the young riders classification lead and in the polka dots angelotti fred writes thank heaven he had some consolation after missing out on the red jersey something he'll be targeting surely deeper into this race and so the climbing continues and gets an awful lot more serious because is our destination and just look at how tough this finish will be lots of mountain points up for grabs all the jerseys could be exchanging hands or indeed held tightly we shall see you
